questions.question: Explain the purpose of FLUSH PRIVILEGES command in MySQL. When is it necessary to use this command?
keyPoints: Reloads grant tables from mysql database
Applies privilege changes immediately without server restart
Required after manual modifications to mysql.user table
Required after GRANT, REVOKE, CREATE USER, DROP USER commands

questions.question: Write the MySQL commands to create a user 'admin' with all privileges on 'college' database who can connect only from localhost.
keyPoints: CREATE USER 'admin'@'localhost' IDENTIFIED BY 'password';
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON college.* TO 'admin'@'localhost';
FLUSH PRIVILEGES;

questions.question: Differentiate between CHECK TABLE, REPAIR TABLE, and OPTIMIZE TABLE commands.
keyPoints: CHECK TABLE - detects errors and corruption in tables
REPAIR TABLE - fixes corrupted MyISAM tables
OPTIMIZE TABLE - defragments tables and reclaims unused space

questions.question: How will you take a backup of a MySQL database named 'university' using mysqldump? Also write the command to restore it.
keyPoints: Backup: mysqldump -u root -p university > university_backup.sql
Restore: mysql -u root -p university < university_backup.sql
Or SOURCE command inside MySQL client

questions.question: Explain the use of SHOW VARIABLES and SHOW STATUS commands in MySQL administration.
keyPoints: SHOW VARIABLES - displays server configuration parameters
SHOW STATUS - displays runtime statistics and performance metrics
Both help in monitoring and tuning database performance

questions.question: Write steps to revoke ALL privileges from a user and then delete the user account completely.
keyPoints: REVOKE ALL PRIVILEGES, GRANT OPTION FROM 'username'@'hostname';
FLUSH PRIVILEGES;
DROP USER 'username'@'hostname';

questions.question: Discuss the importance of database backups. Explain different types of backups (full, incremental, logical, physical) and the MySQL commands used to implement them.
keyPoints: Full backup captures entire database at one point in time
Incremental backup captures only changes since last backup
Logical backup using mysqldump exports SQL statements
Physical backup copies raw database files
mysqldump, mysqlpump, and cold file copy are common methods
--single-transaction for consistent InnoDB backups

questions.question: A database administrator needs to manage user privileges securely. Explain the MySQL privilege system with commands for: (a) Creating users with specific privileges (b) Viewing current privileges (c) Revoking specific privileges (d) Removing user accounts completely.
keyPoints: CREATE USER for new account creation
GRANT for assigning privileges (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, ALL)
SHOW GRANTS FOR to view current privileges
REVOKE to remove specific privileges
DROP USER to delete account
FLUSH PRIVILEGES after modifications

questions.question: Explain the role of a Database Administrator (DBA) in maintaining MySQL database performance. Discuss commands for table maintenance, performance monitoring, and query optimization.
keyPoints: ANALYZE TABLE for updating statistics
OPTIMIZE TABLE for defragmentation
CHECK TABLE and REPAIR TABLE for corruption
SHOW PROCESSLIST for monitoring active queries
SHOW STATUS for performance metrics
EXPLAIN for query execution plans

questions.question: Describe the security aspects of MySQL database administration. Include commands for user authentication, privilege management, and securing the MySQL server.
keyPoints: Strong password policies using ALTER USER or SET PASSWORD
Principle of least privilege - grant only required privileges
Host restriction in user creation (@'localhost' vs @'%')
SSL/TLS for encrypted connections
Firewall restrictions on port 3306
Regular privilege audits using SHOW GRANTS
